reason: The text is Joshua 6, describing the forthcoming destruction of Jericho. God explicitly announces He has given the city and its inhabitants into Joshua's hands (verse 2). The narrative details a supernatural destruction event: the walls will fall (verse 5) when the people shout and priests sound the ram horns. This results in the total destruction of the city's entire population—'all that were in the city, both man and woman, young and old, and ox, and sheep, and ass' (verse 21). This is explicitly not individual but collective annihilation affecting the general public. The destruction is attributed directly to Yahweh's action, presented as both supernatural (the walls collapsing miraculously) and consequential to the covenant community's ritual actions at God's command.
